Tafseer:
1. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

(Indeed Allah has heard the statement of her who disputes with you concerning her husband, and complains to Allah. And Allah hears the argument between you both. Verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Seer) (1)

(Hearing used in the sense of accepting): The context of the discourse proves that (Indeed Allah has heard) does not mean mere listening, but rather accepting and responding. This word is used in this sense both in the Quran and in classical Arabic, and even in our own language, 'hearing' is commonly used to mean accepting. According to the established rules of Arabic grammar, an implied auxiliary verb is present before (disputes with you), which indicates that the lady whose incident is mentioned here had to present herself repeatedly before the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) to explain her case.

(The meaning of Mujadalah): In the Quran, the word 'Mujadalah' is used in both positive and negative senses. In a negative sense, it means stubborn argumentation and quarreling, while in a positive sense, it means attempting to make someone agree to one's point through love, trust, beautiful presentation, reasoning, and persistence. Although there is an outward appearance of dispute in this, it is a dispute born of affection and trust, much like children dispute with their elders to make them agree to something, relying on their affection. The best example of this loving dispute is the one Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him) had with his Lord regarding the people of Lot, which Allah highly praised and which we have detailed in its respective place. The dispute of the lady referred to here is of this very nature. Since there is no single word in Urdu that perfectly captures this positive meaning, I have translated it as 'disputing' or 'arguing,' but this is used in a specific positive sense that is understandable if one keeps the context in view.

(The nature of the incident referred to): According to narrations, the lady referred to in the verse was Khawlah bint Tha'labah (may Allah be pleased with her). Her husband was Aws ibn as-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him), an Ansari. Once in anger, he said to her: 'You are to me like the back of my mother' (meaning: if I touch you from now on, it is as if I have touched my mother's back). In the pre-Islamic period of ignorance (Jahiliyyah), uttering such a statement resulted in a severe form of divorce (Zihar) after which the wife was permanently separated from her husband. This caused great distress to Khawlah, as she worried about where she would go at her age if she were separated from her husband and children. She eventually presented her case to the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), persistently begging him to find a solution to her distress. Since no explicit revelation had been received on this matter yet, the Prophet paused in his reply, which is why she had to repeatedly direct the attention of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) to her issue.

The text of the Quran also indicates that she offered some defense for her husband in this matter. Some narrations suggest that Aws (may Allah be pleased with him) had a somewhat hot-tempered disposition, which led him to utter an inappropriate phrase in anger. His intention was by no means to divorce his wife; hence, both husband and wife faced extreme anxiety. Khawlah must have placed this situation before the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) to make it clear that her husband had no intention of divorce when uttering that phrase, and that it was merely an unintentional slip of the tongue under extreme provocation.

(Answering a question): The practice of Zihar is also mentioned in Surah Al-Ahzab, but only in passing: 'And He has not made your wives whom you declare to be like your mothers' backs (Zihar) your real mothers' (Surah Al-Ahzab: 4). However, first, it is not certain that Surah Al-Ahzab was revealed before Surah Al-Mujadilah. Second, even if it was, it only establishes that Zihar does not make a wife one's mother. It does not clarify what responsibility a person incurs if they commit this act, or what they must do to expiate it. Therefore, even if the verse from Surah Al-Ahzab was before the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), he could only issue a final ruling on Khawlah's case when divine revelation guided him. Thus, he delayed while waiting for divine revelation, until, through the blessing of this lady's complaint and dispute, a blessed way of relief from the consequences of a foolish pre-Islamic custom opened up—not only for her, but for countless servants of Allah. Allah praised her, making her and her dispute immortal, and taught people that whoever faces a difficulty in matters of religion should present their problem to their Lord like this believing woman, and not use it as a pretext for criticism, secret whispering, or opposition against Allah and His Messenger.

(Seeking help from Allah is the key to every difficulty): (And Allah hears the argument between you both) means that since this complaint and dispute were directed particularly toward Allah, He listened to them with special attention and kindness, opening the way to resolve His servant's difficulty. This indicates that those who present their difficulties to their Lord can rest assured that He listens with absolute attention and compassion. He is the All-Hearer, All-Seer, and when He hears and has power over all things, why should a servant despair or harbor doubts about Him?

Remember also that, just like worldly and material problems, the most successful way to emerge from spiritual and intellectual confusion is to present them before one's Lord. Often, one faces an intellectual or rational difficulty for which no solution appears, and doubts begin to arise regarding religion. In such circumstances, if a person presents their problem to their Lord and seeks His guidance, they will achieve reassurance, provided they seek help from their Lord with patience. This has always been the way of the seekers of truth. However, those who are hasty or ignoble, instead of adopting this path, either make their own delusions and doubts their religion, or use them as a means to criticize religion and oppose it, the end of which will be described in detail later in this Surah.

(Those among you who make their wives unlawful to them by Zihar, they are not their mothers. None can be their mothers except those who gave them birth. And verily, they utter an ill word and a lie. And verily, Allah is Oft-Pardoning, Oft-Forgiving) (2).

(Zihar and its legal ruling): The first fundamental principle established in this regard is that when people utter such statements to their wives, their wives do not enter the category of their mothers. Their mothers are only those who gave birth to them. The sanctity and prohibition associated with mothers is based on birth, which is a natural, permanent, and physical prohibition. This status cannot be acquired by any other woman merely because a person compares her or any part of her body to his mother or any part of her body. If anyone utters such a statement, it is a foolish and false word for which he deserves admonition and discipline, but it does not make his wife prohibited to him. (And verily, Allah is Oft-Pardoning, Oft-Forgiving) means that if a Muslim, in a fit of anger, utters such an inappropriate and untrue word and then realizes his mistake, Allah is ready to pardon and forgive. Since the person who committed the error in this case was fully aware of his mistake, Allah pardoned him. Note here that just as the pre-Islamic Arabs gave adopted sons the status of biological sons, they were also extremely rigid regarding Zihar. If a person uttered such a statement, common custom treated his wife as prohibited to him exactly like his mother. No one dared to establish marital relations after Zihar. If anyone ventured to do so, people viewed him as if he had married his own mother. Therefore, in reforming these unnatural customs, Islam points to the reality that everything in the laws of nature must remain in its proper place, and one must not distort the natural religion by altering these positions. This discussion was detailed in the commentary of Surah Al-Ahzab. Here too, in reforming this evil custom and silencing the objectors, it is first clarified that unnatural utterances do not alter the laws of nature. A wife does not become a mother merely due to a husband's false words.

(A grammatical question): A question arises regarding the grammatical case of the word (their mothers) in (they are not their mothers) as to why it is in the accusative case. In my view, the particle 'ma' here functions like 'laysa' (negation). Examples of this are present in the Quran, such as in Surah Al-Haqqah: 'Then none of you could withhold Us from him' (47).

(And those who make Zihar from their wives and then wish to go back on what they have uttered, then the freeing of a slave before they touch each other. That is an admonition to you, and Allah is All-Aware of what you do) (3).

(The expiation of Zihar): This is the solution provided: whoever commits Zihar with his wife and then wishes to return to what he declared prohibited must free a slave as expiation before they touch each other. That is, his wife will not become prohibited to him like his mother due to Zihar, but since the consequences of marriage and divorce are far-reaching in social life, serious and jesting utterances in this regard carry great weight. This demands that such a person be disciplined so that he adopts caution in the future and others learn a lesson from him. Thus, it is necessary for him to free a slave before touching his wife. The word 'Raqabah' is used here, which means a neck, indicating that there is no distinction between a male or female slave. Some jurists do not even consider the condition of belief or disbelief necessary; whichever is available will suffice for the expiation, though a Muslim slave is preferred if available. Note that freeing a slave is placed first. If a slave is not available, the other alternative forms mentioned below may be adopted. This supports our view expressed in the commentary of Surah An-Nur that Islam opened very wide paths for freeing slaves within its system, making it the expiation for many major and minor sins to strengthen this objective from every angle.

(Ambiguity in referring to the forbidden act): The phrase (then wish to go back on what they have uttered) contains some ambiguity. The reason for this ambiguity is that the act referred to is obviously inappropriate, and the Quran has termed it an ill word and a lie. Explicitly mentioning an inappropriate act is not suitable, so the Quran referred to it in ambiguous terms. The meaning is simply that if they wish to do the very thing they declared prohibited after Zihar, they are commanded to free a slave before touching each other. This ambiguity is resolved first by the words (before they touch each other), and then the identical style is used in verse 8 of this Surah: 'and then they return to what they were forbidden.' Similarly, here it means that they return to the act they pledged to avoid or declared prohibited. A fine example of the ambiguity that occurs in words like 'they said' or 'they say' for the sake of eloquence and brevity can be found in Surah Maryam, verse 80. For further details, refer to its commentary.

In the phrase (before they touch each other), the emphasis is on paying the expiation before any physical contact occurs. This condition is repeated in the subsequent verse, showing its emphasized nature; hence, it is not permissible to yield to physical desire and establish relations before paying the expiation. If this is done, it will be a transgression of the limits of Allah, as mentioned below.

(Admonition): (That is an admonition to you, and Allah is All-Aware of what you do) is a warning that this advice is given to you by your Lord. If you violate it secretly or openly, remember that Allah is aware of all your actions. Since He is aware, you cannot escape His grasp. 'That' (Zalikum) refers to all the instructions mentioned above. That is, do not persist in pre-Islamic customs regarding Zihar, and do not invent loopholes and legal excuses to escape the expiation; rather, fulfill every command in its true spirit. In this lies success in both this world and the next.

(And he who finds not must fast two successive months before they touch each other. And he who is unable to do so must feed sixty poor persons. That is so that you may have perfect faith in Allah and His Messenger. These are the limits set by Allah. And for the disbelievers is a painful torment) (4). That is, if a slave is not available, one must fast for two consecutive months, and if unable, one must feed sixty poor persons. In modern times, practically only these two forms remain, since slavery has ended, which is in complete harmony with the objectives of Islam. The word 'successive' (mutatabi'ayn) indicates that if marital relations occur before the completion of the two months of fasting, one must start the fasting all over again. Although the condition (before they touch each other) is not explicitly repeated with (feeding sixty poor persons), it is understood in this option as well. It was not mentioned simply because this option is an alternative and not the primary command; since it was mentioned with the primary, it was unnecessary to repeat it with the alternative. (That is so that you may have perfect faith in Allah and His Messenger) states the benefit of these instructions: they are given so that your faith in Allah and His Messenger becomes firm. (To believe) is used here in its complete sense according to the classical style of Arabic, as explained previously. If a person undertakes a hardship to expiate for a mistake or weakness, it expiates the mistake and firmly establishes their steps in the original objective.

(These are the limits set by Allah) is a warning similar to (That is an admonition to you) mentioned above; that is, these are the boundaries established by Allah. Do not venture to transgress them, or the end will be extremely evil. (And for the disbelievers is a painful torment) means that those who violate Allah's limits are disbelievers, and for them in the Hereafter is a painful punishment. Unfortunate are those who, while claiming Islam, place themselves among the disbelievers. There are other juristic questions regarding Zihar, but they belong to the domain of jurisprudence (Fiqh) rather than exegesis; hence, we will not address them here. Those seeking details may refer to books of jurisprudence.

2. Subject Matter of Verses 5–11

Following the discussion on positive dispute, the verses now describe the character of those who were diseased with hostility against Islam and the Messenger. Although these people were outwardly included among the Muslims, they were agents of the Jews, working as their representatives within the Muslim community. Their specific method was to use any pretext to whisper among themselves against Islam and the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) within the Muslim gatherings, aiming to shake the faith of the Muslims and make them despair of the future of Islam. Here, their conspiracies are unveiled, and they are warned that Allah is fully aware of their every secret whisper, and its consequence will soon face them. At the same time, Muslims are taught some measures to remain safe from their evil and to keep the assembly of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) pure from their whispering. Recite the verses.

3. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

(Verily, those who oppose Allah and His Messenger will be disgraced as those before them were disgraced. And We have indeed sent down clear proofs. And for the disbelievers is a disgraceful torment) (5).

(The meaning of Muhaddah): 'Muhaddah' means opposition and hostility. This word is the opposite of 'Muwaddah' (affection). In this very Surah, both words are used beautifully in a single verse, shedding light on each other's meaning: 'You will not find any people who believe in Allah and the Last Day making friendship with those who oppose Allah and His Messenger' (verse 22).

(The character of the false claimants of Islam): The preceding section described the character of those who, when facing a difficulty in religion, presented it to Allah and His Messenger, directing their complaints and arguments to them. Now, in contrast, the verses describe the character of those who claimed faith and Islam but maintained an attitude of opposition to Allah and His Messenger. Any instruction of Islam that they felt went against their interests or desires became a target of their secret whispering and whispering campaigns, aiming to introduce doubts into the hearts of the sincere Muslims and shake their faith. Their true sympathies, as explained later, lay with the enemies of Islam, particularly the Jews. They acted as agents of the Jews within the Muslim community, working under their guidance to exploit any opportunity to harm the Muslims. The details of the trials they instigated regarding Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) and Zaynab (may Allah be pleased with her) have been discussed in the commentary of Surah An-Nur and Surah Al-Ahzab. The issue of Zihar was also among those matters where they could target the concessions of the Quran to spread doubts among the simple-minded. In the period of ignorance, as indicated, people treated Zihar as a severe divorce. These evil individuals could easily whisper among people that Islam had permitted marriage with one's mother. This situation demanded that these wicked people be thoroughly exposed at this juncture so that any attempt to raise a trial could be prevented.

(The end of the opponents of the Messenger): (Will be disgraced as those before them were disgraced): 'Kabt' means to destroy through humiliation and disgrace. It is stated that these people will be destroyed in disgrace just as those of their mindset before them were destroyed. (And We have indeed sent down clear proofs) refers to the historical realities detailed in the Quran to clarify that those who oppose Allah's Messengers are ultimately destroyed in disgrace. (And for the disbelievers is a disgraceful torment) warns that a humiliating punishment awaits these disbelievers who were active in opposing the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). It states that they will ultimately be disgraced just as their predecessors were. Since their character is identical, there is no reason for their history to be different; Allah's law is the same for all. This threat was fulfilled when the power of Quraysh was broken and the Jews met their end. At that point, no place of refuge remained for the hypocrites who conspired under Jewish patronage; they met the same fate as their Jewish mentors. The exemplary details of their end appear in the subsequent Surahs.

Their punishment is termed 'disgraceful' because they faced humiliating execution, exile, and enslavement, and no one felt any sympathy for them; instead, everyone cursed them.

(On the Day when Allah will resurrect them all together and inform them of what they did. Allah has kept account of it, while they have forgotten it. And Allah is Witness over all things) (6). Although the word 'Day' (Yawma) can grammatically connect to 'disgraceful torment,' in my view, according to the established rules of Arabic grammar, it is in the accusative case governed by an implied verb. The meaning is that whatever they face in this world will occur, but they must also remember the Day when Allah will resurrect them all together and place everything they did before them. The condition 'all together' (jami'an) points to the reality that all the participants and factors of today's conspiracies and whispers will be gathered, and Allah will unveil all their secrets before everyone, showing who gave what advice and who executed it. 'He will inform them' obviously implies its consequence—meaning Allah will make them face the results of all their deeds.

(Warning against a delusion): (Allah has kept account of it, while they have forgotten it) warns against a major delusion: they must not assume that because they have forgotten what they did, Allah has also forgotten it. Since they did not face immediate punishment for their mischief, they assumed Allah had forgotten, but Allah has recorded their every movement and has appointed a Day of judgment when everyone's good and evil deeds will be placed before them. (And Allah is Witness over all things) is the evidence for this: Allah is never absent; rather, whatever occurs in the heavens and the earth happens before Him, and He is present everywhere.

(Have you not seen that Allah knows whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is on the earth? There is no secret counsel of three but He is their fourth, nor of five but He is their sixth, nor of less than that or more but He is with them wheresoever they may be. Morever on the Day of Resurrection He will inform them of what they did. Verily, Allah is the All-Knower of everything) (7).

(Allah is present everywhere): This is a further explanation of the preceding point: Allah is aware of every single thing in the heavens and the earth. He will place every person's deeds before them. The phrase 'Have you not seen' (Alam tara) is general, pointing to the reality that this is a truth that should be obvious to everyone. Since Allah is the Creator of the heavens and the earth and operates this entire system, and every movement of its parts occurs by His permission, and the life of every living thing is by His command, how can anything in the heavens and the earth remain hidden from Him? 'Should He not know what He has created?' (Surah Al-Mulk: 14).

(There is no secret counsel of three...): It is stated that since Allah's knowledge encompasses all things, those who whisper against Allah and His Messenger must remember that when three of them whisper, Allah is their fourth, and when five of them whisper, Allah is their sixth, and whether they are less or more, He is inevitably with them. (Wheresoever they may be) warns that wherever they hide to whisper, Allah is present with them. They cannot find any corner in the heavens or the earth to hide themselves from the God Who knows the unseen.

## 1. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ نُهُوا عَنِ النَّجْوَىٰ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِمَا نُهُوا عَنْهُ وَيَتَنَاجَوْنَ بِالإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَمَعْصِيَتِ الرَّسُولِ وَإِذَا جَاءُوكَ حَيَّوكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ وَيَقُولُونَ فِي أَنفُسِهِمْ لَوْلا يُعَذِّبُنَا اللَّهُ بِمَا نَقُولُ حَسْبُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ يَصْلَوْنَهَا فَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ)**

*(Have you not seen those who were forbidden from secret counsels, yet they return to what they were forbidden from, and hold secret counsels for sin, transgression, and disobedience to the Messenger? And when they come to you, they greet you with a greeting that Allah does not greet you with, and say within themselves, "Why does Allah not punish us for what we say?" Sufficient for them is Hell, which they will enter to burn—and wretched is the destination.)*

### The Real Purpose of Secret Counsels
This expresses astonishment at their state—look at their audacity that they repeatedly commit the very act of *Najwa* (secret counsel) they were forbidden from. An even greater audacity is that they whisper and scheme about sin, transgression, and disobedience to the Messenger, feeling neither shame for their actions nor any fear of Allah. It appears that when their whispering began, the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) drew attention to their behavior, but it had no effect on them; instead, their mischief only intensified. Ultimately, these verses delivered a final warning to them.

***

### Whispering for Sin, Transgression, and Disobedience
The phrase **(وَيَتَنَاجَوْنَ بِالإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَمَعْصِيَتِ الرَّسُولِ)** means that their secret conversations contain no element of goodness at all. Although secret counsel can sometimes be for a good cause—as mentioned later—their hearts are entirely devoid of virtue. Consequently, every instance of their whispering is meant to incite sin, aggression, and rebellion against the Messenger.

* ***Ithm* (Sin):** Refers to those sins in which the violation of others' rights is prominent.
* ***Udwan* (Transgression):** Applies to those sins characterized by defiance, rebellion, and crossing boundaries.

When these two words are used together, they encompass all categories of sin. The words **(مَعْصِيَتِ الرَّسُولِ)** (disobedience to the Messenger) are used here to pinpoint the actual target of these miscreants' activities: their entire struggle was aimed at inciting rebellion among Muslims against the Messenger of Allah to dismantle their unity. As indicated above, these hypocrites were groomed and manipulated by the Jews, and the ultimate objective of all their operations was to instigate discord among the Companions before this group could solidify into an invincible power. The details of their conspiracies have appeared earlier in this book and will feature again, but Allah disgraced them, and the *Hizbullah* (Party of Allah) organized under the guidance of Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah, prevailed over all enemies of the truth.

***

### The Malice of the Miscreants
The verse states: **(وَإِذَا جَاءُوكَ حَيَّوكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ)**. This provides an example of the deep-seated malice these wicked people harbored against the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). When they come to you, they greet you using words completely opposite to what Allah has chosen for you. While Allah and His angels send blessings and peace upon you, these wretched souls deliberately distort the word *As-Salam* when greeting you, so that instead of *As-Salamu Alaykum*, it comes out corrupted.

The phrase **(بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ)** (with a greeting Allah does not greet you with) is intended to highlight their wretchedness. What doubt can remain about the misfortune and deprivation of people who invoke death upon the holy persona upon whom Allah and His angels send blessings and peace? This also contains a message of consolation for the Prophet: if they wish for your death, let them; when the mercy of God and the prayers of His angels are with you, why should you care about anyone else's prayers or curses?

The ways the Jews and their agents twisted their tongues to insult the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) by distorting words were discussed in the commentary of Surah Al-Baqarah under the terms *Ra'ina* and *Sam'ina wa Ata'ina*. They carried out a similar manipulation with the word *As-Salam*, turning it into *As-Sam* (meaning death in Arabic), so that the listener was tricked into thinking it was *As-Salamu Alaykum*, while they were actually saying *As-Samu Alaykum* (death be upon you).

***

### The Core Misconception of the Wicked
The verse continues: **(وَيَقُولُونَ فِي أَنفُسِهِمْ لَوْلا يُعَذِّبُنَا اللَّهُ بِمَا نَقُولُ)**. This points to the delusion that made them bolder in their mischief. Because they are not immediately seized for their villainy, they believe they are completely justified in denying and mocking the Messenger. They think that if he were truly a Messenger of Allah, we would surely have been punished for these words; since nothing of the sort is happening, it means his claim is merely an empty threat.

Allah responds: **(حَسْبُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ يَصْلَوْنَهَا فَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ)** (Sufficient for them is Hell, which they will enter to burn—and wretched is the destination). Hell alone is enough for them, into which they will certainly be cast. Even if punishment does not overtake them in this world, it makes no difference; Hell is entirely sufficient to settle the score. Although these miscreants are also threatened with worldly punishment—as stated in verse 5 and coming up later—the ultimate reality remains that with Hell waiting, even if someone escapes worldly accountability, it offers no grounds for complacency.

It must be kept in mind that nothing fuels human negligence and rebellion more than this very misconception. When a person sees that his misdeeds go unpunished in this world, he assumes he will never be held accountable anywhere, and that the warnings of the preachers are just empty rhetoric. This is nothing but a total lack of insight. If the consequences of actions were manifested instantly, no one would ever dare to commit evil; everyone would become righteous and pious out of sheer necessity. The real secret of this world is that it is not the Domain of Recompense (*Dar al-Jaza*) but the Domain of Testing (*Dar al-Imtihan*). The Domain of Recompense lies ahead, and it is the most distinct, immutable reality of this universe. Without it, this entire world would turn into an unjust realm and a mere playground, whereas the Creator of this universe is certainly no player, but is self-evidently All-Mighty and All-Wise.

## 2. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا تَنَاجَيْتُمْ فَلا تَتَنَاجَوْا بِالإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَمَعْصِيَتِ الرَّسُولِ وَتَنَاجَوْا بِالْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوَىٰ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ)**

*(O you who have believed, when you converse privately, do not converse for sin and aggression and disobedience to the Messenger but converse for righteousness and piety. And fear Allah, to whom you will be gathered.)*

### The Pure Category of Secret Counsel
This is a guidance for the Muslims that *Najwa* (private consultation) is not a bad thing in itself. In collective and social life, situations arise where confidential consultation becomes necessary. Mutual consultation (*Shura*) is among the foundations of collective life, and the need for confidentiality can present itself. If this consultation is for righteousness, piety, and reconciling relationships, then such *Najwa* is a source of goodness and blessing. Whenever you engage in a confidential consultation, do it for a virtuous objective. 

Conversely, that *Najwa* is satanic which these hypocrites conduct for the purpose of sin, transgression, and disobedience to the Messenger. The believers must avoid it and remain fearful of that Allah before whom everyone will be gathered, and Who is fully aware of everyone's secrets, as explained above.

## 3. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(إِنَّمَا النَّجْوَىٰ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ لِيَحْزُنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَيْسَ بِضَارِّهِمْ شَيْئًا إِلا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ)**

*(Private conversation is only from Satan that he may grieve those who have believed, but he will not harm them at all except by permission of Allah. And upon Allah let the believers rely.)*

### Consolation for the Believers
This offers consolation to the sincere believers that all these whisperings of the hypocrites originate from the instigation and insinuations of Satan. The most Satan can achieve through such tactics is to cause temporary grief and distress to the hearts of the believers; anything beyond that is outside his power. He cannot cause the slightest harm to the believers without the permission of Allah. Therefore, the believers should pay no attention to these satanic whisperings, stand firm in their position, and trust their Lord, Who will protect them from the mischief of every malicious entity.

## 4. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا فِي الْمَجَالِسِ فَافْسَحُوا يَفْسَحِ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَإِذَا قِيلَ انشُزُوا فَانشُزُوا يَرْفَعِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ دَرَجَاتٍ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ)**

*(O you who have believed, when it is said to you, "Make room in assemblies," then make room; Allah will make room for you. And when it is said, "Arise," then arise; Allah will raise those who have believed among you and those who were given knowledge, by degrees. And Allah is Acquainted with what you do.)*

### Instructions to Cleanse the Prophetic Assembly of Secret Whispers
Although the whisperings of the hypocrites occurred in every gathering, they made special arrangements for this mischief in the Prophetic assemblies. This was because it offered them the greatest opportunities to criticize Islam, cause emotional distress to Muslims, and disrespect or mock the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). Consequently, they would sit in the Prophetic assembly forming their own exclusive cliques, ensuring no outsider could enter their circle so they could easily exchange critiques, sneers, and sarcastic remarks regarding the Prophet's words.

Frequently, they would remain huddled together even after the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) left or the conclusion of the assembly was announced, staying behind to plant doubts among the people or map out new mischief. This state of affairs necessitated certain regulations for the Prophetic assembly to prevent the hypocrites from exploiting it for their satanic whisperings.

***

### Some Etiquettes of Assemblies
The directive **(إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا فِي الْمَجَالِسِ فَافْسَحُوا)** primarily targets the assembly of the Prophet, but the use of the plural form *Al-Majalis* indicates that the etiquettes taught here apply to all assemblies of Muslims, so that every gathering reflects the assembly of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). Similarly, the word *Masajid* is used in the plural in some places in the Qur'an even when referring primarily to the Masjid al-Haram—as in *"Innama ya'muru masajida Allah"* (Surah At-Tawbah: 18)—to teach that the instructions given for the central mosque apply to all mosques of Allah, since they are all subordinate to it.

The meaning is that when the leader of the assembly gives a direction to make room, it must be obeyed without hesitation. People should adjust to accommodate others so that latecomers can easily find a place, thereby reducing the space available for the hypocrites to form cliques and whisper among themselves. As indicated above, the hypocrites sat in the Prophetic assembly like a political party, making sure no one from outside could enter their circle to prevent any interference with their reactions or any exposure of their signs, whispers, and sarcastic remarks. This behavior caused major complications at times, as recorded in history books and hinted at in the Qur'an. During the period of this Surah's revelation, this fitnah had escalated, prompting the command to make room upon request.

***

### The Divine Promise of Elevation
Allah says: **(فَافْسَحُوا يَفْسَحِ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ)** (make room; Allah will make room for you). While the apparent meaning implies that Allah will provide spaciousness for you in Paradise, the reality is that when a person keeps his heart open for others, Allah opens the hearts of others for him in this world as well. Every action, whether external or internal, good or bad, carries a natural consequence whose true manifestation will be in the Hereafter, but its fruits can be observed in this world too, provided one has the eyes to see.

Similarly, **(وَإِذَا قِيلَ انشُزُوا فَانشُزُوا)** means that if the leader of the assembly gives a directive to stand up or disperse, it must be carried out instantly without any sense of offense. This command can be both collective and individual. Collectively, if the entire assembly is asked to disperse, people should accept it cheerfully without feeling that their time was undervalued, that personal comfort was prioritized over collective work, or that they were denied an opportunity to express their views.

Individually, if someone is directed to vacate his spot to move elsewhere, leave the room, or make space for someone else, he must comply without objection. If he accepts this humbling out of respect for collective discipline, Allah will turn this sacrifice into a means of elevation. The phrase **(فَانشُزُوا يَرْفَعِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ)** stands in direct parallel to the promise of spaciousness; even if moving appears degrading on the surface, Allah will turn it into a source of honor and elevation for those who truly believe.

The wording **(الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ دَرَجَاتٍ)** is highly meaningful. The hypocrites embedded among you will react with severe resentment to such orders, feeling insulted that they were asked to move for someone else, or questioning whether they were inferior in knowledge or status to those given preference. However, the true believers and people of knowledge who execute this command with a pleasant heart will find their ranks elevated higher and higher by Allah in the Hereafter.

Finally, **(وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ)** consoles these people of faith and knowledge that Allah is fully aware of their actions. He does not need your sacrifices to be publicized in newspapers to know about them; He knows every sacrifice you make to maintain religious discipline and respect the organization, and He will reward it fully.

***

### The Condition of Contemporary Assemblies
Although this guidance was originally given for the Prophetic assembly, these etiquettes ought to be observed in all assemblies of Muslims. Today, blind imitation of Western methods has turned our parliaments and councils into arenas where opposing factions arrive solely to defeat one another, exchange gestures, whisper, and trade insults. At times, this degenerates into physical altercations, forcing the chairperson to call for law enforcement rather than relying on the etiquettes of the assembly.

## 5. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا نَاجَيْتُمُ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَةً ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ وَأَطْهَرُ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَجِدُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ)**

*(O you who have believed, when you [wish to] privately consult the Messenger, introduce your private consultation with a charity. That is better for you and purer. But if you find not [the means]—then indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.)*

### A Temporary Decree to Curb Private Audiences
Just as the preceding verses introduced measures to protect the Prophetic assembly from disruptive whispering, this verse outlines a directive to protect the persona of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) from exploitative private audiences. When this epidemic of whispering grew and was censured in the Qur'an, many hypocrites sought private audiences with the Prophet to offer smooth explanations and shift the blame for their actions onto innocent people. Because they lacked the courage to clear themselves in public, every one of them tried to secure private meetings with the Prophet.

This situation made it necessary to place certain conditions on meeting him privately so that people would stop wasting his valuable time. This was a temporary order that was abrogated once its purpose was achieved. The abrogating verse was placed immediately after the initial command in the textual order of the Qur'an to preserve the coherence of the discourse.

The restriction required anyone wishing to speak with the Prophet in private to give something in charity beforehand. Poor Muslims were exempted from this rule so it would not become a burden for them, and no fixed amount was set for the charity to keep it manageable. The primary objective was to discourage the hypocrites' growing habit of whispering. The diseases of stinginess and love of wealth were widespread among the hypocrites, and the Qur'an has highlighted this vulnerability in numerous places. 

By adding a financial condition, private audiences with the Prophet became a serious matter, ensuring that these characters would either refrain out of stinginess or, if they did spend, the charity would serve to purify their hearts and steer them away from malicious talk. The phrase **(ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ وَأَطْهَرُ)** implies that if you act upon this with sincere intentions, it will bring goodness in this world and the Next, serving as a means to purify your hearts. It also signals that this command was not given out of a desire for the Prophet’s personal luxury, but for your own rectitude and success. The conclusion, **(فَإِنْ لَمْ تَجِدُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ)**, leaves the door wide open for the poor and needy to meet the Prophet privately and present their matters as usual.

## 6. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَنْ تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَاتٍ فَإِذْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا وَتَابَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَأَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَاللَّهُ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ)**

*(Have you feared to introduce your private consultation with charities? Then when you do not do it and Allah has forgiven you, then establish prayer and give zakah and obey Allah and His Messenger. And Allah is Acquainted with what you do.)*

### The Conjoining of the Abrogating and Abrogated Verses
This verse was revealed some time later to abrogate the command of the previous verse, but it was placed right next to the original command within the Surah so that the abrogated and abrogating rules sit together. Naturally, a sufficient interval must have passed between the two verses for the initial command to achieve its purpose—meaning the fitnah of the hypocrites' whispering had subsided. Although some explanatory reports imply that the interval was as short as a single day, this assumption likely stems from the close proximity of the two verses in the text. There are multiple instances in the Qur'an where an abrogating verse was revealed much later but placed right next to the abrogated command in the final compilation, such as in Surah Al-Muzzammil.

The restriction on private meetings was introduced to deal with a specific crisis at a particular time, and it would only have been lifted once the mischief was contained or the people had developed a collective sense of self-restraint. Such an effect cannot be produced in a matter of hours; it requires a reasonable amount of time. Therefore, a logical gap exists between the revelation of the two verses.

The words **(أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَنْ تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَاتٍ)** indicate that the obsession with seeking private audiences dropped significantly after the command was issued. The hypocrites stopped out of stinginess, while the sincere believers became highly cautious, realizing that holding private consultations with the Prophet without pressing need was displeasing to Allah. Once the objective of this temporary measure was achieved, the restriction was removed.

The phrase **(فَإِذْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا وَتَابَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ)** shows that people refrained from private conversations to avoid having to give charity, and it indicates that a collective sense of remorse developed among them, realizing that their previous approach had not been pleasing to Allah. The phrase *"Taba Allahu alaykum"* means Allah accepted your repentance. This divine grace turned toward them because their remorse made them worthy of forgiveness. Even if a few hard-hearted individuals remained unchanged, the verse shows that a collective sense of accountability emerged within the community. It is the sunnah of Allah that when a positive change takes hold within a community as a whole, He accepts it and lifts the restrictive trial.

As a closing safeguard, Allah says: **(فأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ)**. Even though this restriction is lifted, you must pay extra attention to establishing prayer, giving zakah, and obeying Allah and His Messenger, so that such a critical situation does not arise in society again and Satan finds no opportunity to revive the fitnah of whispering. Always keep the reality in mind: **(وَاللَّهُ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ)** (And Allah is Acquainted with what you do).

## 7. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا قَوْمًا غَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مَا هُمْ مِنْكُمْ وَلَا مِنْهُمْ وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ)**

*(Have you not seen those who make allies of a people with whom Allah has become angry? They are neither of you nor of them, and they swear to untruth while they know [they are lying].)*

### Hypocrites Stand Only for Their Own Interests
This verse expresses astonishment at the state of the hypocrites: on one hand, they form alliances with those who have earned the wrath of Allah, and on the other, they swear oaths to Muslims, claiming to be part of them. Allah clarifies: **(مَا هُمْ مِنْكُمْ وَلَا مِنْهُمْ)** (They are neither of you nor of them). They are not with you because faith in Allah and His Messenger cannot coexist with an alliance with those who have earned His wrath; these two positions are completely incompatible. They are not genuinely with their allies either, because their alliance is based purely on self-interest. If their allies face a major crisis, these hypocrites will not step forward to bear any hardship for them; instead, they will distance themselves and flee, just like Satan.

The verse continues: **(وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ)**. They know very well that they stand with no one but themselves, yet they take false oaths to keep both sides pleased. The phrase **(غَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ)** (those with whom Allah has become angry) refers to the Jews, who are explicitly designated as *"Al-Maghdubi Alayhim"* (those who earned wrath) in the very first Surah of the Qur'an. Although the hypocrites also maintained ties with the polytheists of Makkah—as detailed in later Surahs—the specific reference here is to their covert alliance with the Jews. As noted earlier, these hypocrites were largely set up and manipulated by the Jews, operating as their agents inside the Muslim community.

## 8. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا إِنَّهُمْ سَاءَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ)**

*(Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. Indeed, it is evil that they used to do.)*

These unfortunate individuals imagine they are practicing clever politics by keeping both sides satisfied through their false oaths. In reality, this is not clever diplomacy but a fatal game. Consequently, Allah has prepared a severe punishment for them. They will face their share of the worldly destruction destined for the enemies of Allah and His Messenger, and the torment awaiting them in the Hereafter is certain.

## 9. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُهِينٌ)**

*(They will have taken their oaths as a cover, so they averted [people] from the way of Allah, and for them is a humiliating punishment.)*

### Hiding Behind False Oaths
They have turned their oaths into a shield (*Junnah*). Whenever any of their faults are questioned, they use false oaths to offer reassurances, believing they have successfully protected themselves. They use these oaths to create a hiding place, shielding themselves from the demands of faith. By professing faith, they initially took a step toward the path of Allah, but then they halted, using oaths to maintain a false appearance of piety. How long can they sustain this facade? Ultimately, a humiliating punishment awaits them.

The word *Sadd* is used in the Qur'an both transitively and intransitively. Here it is used in the intransitive sense, meaning they themselves stopped and held back from advancing further on the path of Allah.

## 10. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(لَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ)**

*(Never will their wealth or their children avail them against Allah at all. Those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.)*

This points to the specific chains binding their feet, preventing them from advancing on the path of Allah. They are trapped by their love for wealth, properties, and family, which leaves them with no courage to face the demands of faith. They do not realize that their wealth and children will not protect them from the punishment of Allah in the slightest. In the phrase **(مِنَ اللَّهِ)**, a word is understood: *"min 'adhab Allah"* (against the punishment of Allah). If they remain consumed by this material love, it will lead them straight to Hell, from which they will never escape.

## 11. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللَّهُ جَمِيعًا فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ كَمَا يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْكَاذِبُونَ)**

*(On the Day Allah will resurrect them all, and they will swear to Him as they swear to you and think that they are [standing] on something. Unquestionably, it is they who are the liars.)*

If they continue to evade the requirements of faith out of attachment to wealth and children, they should remember the Day Allah will resurrect them along with their children. On that Day, they will have no recourse left except the same false oaths they rely on today. They will swear false oaths before their Lord to assert their innocence, just as they do before you. Surah Al-An'am (verse 23) describes the polytheists that when they face the punishment, they will swear to their innocence, saying: *"Wallahi Rabbina ma kunna mushrikin"* (By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists).

The phrase **(وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ)** indicates that by taking a false oath, they will imagine they have presented a strong argument for their innocence. They think that since their deception worked on many people in this world, it will work in the Hereafter as well. However, their deception will fail them completely there. On that Day, Allah will seal the mouths of the criminals, and their hands, feet, and other limbs will bear witness to their crimes, leaving no room for excuses.

Allah concludes: **(أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْكَاذِبُونَ)**. Let everyone listen carefully: if anyone has mistakenly assumed them to be truthful, they should dismiss that thought. These are the ultimate liars who deceive people here with their falsehood and will have the audacity to try to deceive their Lord in the Hereafter.

## 12. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(اسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ الشَّيْطَانُ فَأَنْسَاهُمْ ذِكْرَ اللَّهِ أُولَٰئِكَ حِزْبُ الشَّيْطَانِ أَلَا إِنَّ حِزْبَ الشَّيْطَانِ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ)**

*(Satan has overcome them and made them forget the remembrance of Allah. Those are the party of Satan. Unquestionably, the party of Satan—they are the losers.)*

### Forgetting God Opens the Door to Satan
The phrase **(اسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ)** means *"tasallata alayhim"*—Satan has established complete control over them. When Satan gains complete control over people, he makes them entirely unmindful of the remembrance of Allah, and those who forget God naturally end up joining Satan's party. It must be understood that while Satan tries his whispers on everyone, his control is established only over those who open their hearts to his insinuations and accept him as their guide. Once people become his followers, the remembrance of God vanishes from their hearts; they become completely indifferent to their ultimate fate and hand their reins over to him. Such individuals become members of Satan’s party, and he leads them wherever he wishes. The real shield for a human being is the remembrance of God; once deprived of it, a person falls into Satan's hands and struggles to break free.

***

### Failure is Decreed for Satan's Party
Allah states: **(أَلَا إِنَّ حِزْبَ الشَّيْطَانِ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ)**. Although everyone is granted a specific respite under Allah's law in this world, allowing Satan's party the time to try their hand, let it be known to all that Satan's party will ultimately face absolute failure.

## 13. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحَادُّونَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ أُولَٰئِكَ فِي الْأَذَلِّينَ)**

*(Indeed, the ones who oppose Allah and His Messenger—those will be among the most humbled.)*

The primary business of Satan's party is to oppose Allah and His Messenger and create a front against them. The verse states that despite the respite they are given to fight against Allah and His Messenger, they will ultimately be included among the disgraced nations who opposed Him in the past and were humiliated.

## 14. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ)**

*(Allah has decreed, "I will surely overcome, I and My messengers." Indeed, Allah is Powerful and Exalted in Might.)*

### The Divine Sunnah Regarding Messengers
Allah here announces His eternal law, written since the beginning: in the conflict between Allah's Messengers and the party of Satan, victory belongs to Allah and His Messengers, while Satan's party will face humiliation. This divine law operates consistently: whenever a Messenger is sent to a nation, he arrives as the embodiment of divine justice for that people, after which a final decision becomes inevitable. 

If the nation as a whole rejects the Messenger, Allah extracts the Messenger and the few believers, then destroys the rest of the nation through a natural disaster. If the number of believers is substantial, they are commanded to fight against the enemies of truth to break their power and cleanse the earth of their corruption. 

In the history of the Messengers, the first scenario occurred most frequently—the nation was destroyed by a divine affliction following the migration of the Messenger. In the case of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), the second scenario took place: he was commanded to engage in jihad after migration, and his enemies were defeated by the swords of his Companions until their power was completely dismantled.

The verse concludes with the rationale for this inevitable victory: **(إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ)** (Indeed, Allah is Powerful and Exalted in Might). Allah is not a weak entity; He is All-Powerful and Mighty. A Messenger is the ambassador of Allah, arriving to communicate the decrees of the true Sovereign. If people submit to the commands of their Creator, He grants them authority on earth and showers them with blessings; if they choose to fight against Allah, they are designated as rebels, and Allah cleanses His earth of them once the argument is fully established.

## 15. Analysis of Words and Explanation of Verses

**(لَا تَجِدُ قَوْمًا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ يُوَادُّونَ مَنْ حَادَّ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلَوْ كَانُوا آبَاءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوَانَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ أُولَٰئِكَ كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ أُولَٰئِكَ حِزْبُ اللَّهِ أَلَا إِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ)**

*(You will not find a people who believe in Allah and the Last Day having affection for those who oppose Allah and His Messenger, even if they were their fathers or their sons or their brothers or their kindred. Those—He has decreed within their hearts faith and supported them with spirit from Him. And We will admit them to gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him—those are the party of Allah. Unquestionably, the party of Allah—they are the successful.)*

### A Clear Criterion for the Hypocrites
In conclusion, a clear criterion is placed before the hypocrites so that no confusion remains between *Hizbullah* (the Party of Allah) and *Hizb ush-Shaitan* (the Party of Satan), allowing them to see exactly whose side they are on. They can choose only one of the two paths; it is impossible to walk with both simultaneously. It is impossible for a people to truly believe in Allah and the Last Day while harboring love for those who are actively fighting Allah and His Messenger. If a group claims to have faith while maintaining close friendships with the opponents of Allah and His Messenger, their claim to faith is false. These two positions are mutually exclusive. A person possesses only one heart, not two, and cannot accommodate love for two opposing, warring entities.

The verse specifies: **(وَلَوْ كَانُوا آبَاءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوَانَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ)**. Putting aside lesser relationships, even the closest bonds of fathers, sons, brothers, and kindred—to which a person is naturally attached—cannot take precedence if they conflict with faith. A true believer will not allow them to compromise his commitment to God.

***

### Divine Endorsement and Spiritual Support
Allah says of them: **(أُولَٰئِكَ كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ)**. Those who stand true to this test are the ones in whose hearts Allah has firmly inscribed faith and whom He has strengthened with a special spiritual light. As for those who claim faith but maintain covert understandings with the enemies of Allah and His Messenger, their Islam remains confined to their tongues; their hearts have not known true faith. The words **(وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ)** point to a profound reality: cutting ties with fathers, sons, brothers, and tribe—bonds woven into the very fabric of the human heart—is no easy task. However, when those who carry a genuine sense of honor for their faith encounter such a delicate trial, Allah grants them strength through His special spiritual grace, enabling them to emerge from the trial successful.

It was a manifestation of this spiritual grace when Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah faced his father Abdullah al-Jarrah at the Battle of Uhud, when Abu Bakr challenged his own son at Badr, when Mus'ab ibn Umayr slew his brother Ubayd ibn Umayr at Uhud, when Umar ibn al-Khattab executed his maternal uncle As ibn Hisham, and when Ali, Hamzah, and Abu Ubaidah brought an end to Utbah, Shaybah, and Walid ibn Utbah.

***

### The Ultimate Reward: Divine Pleasure
Their reward is stated as: **(وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا)**. In life, Allah blessed them with firmly rooted faith and spiritual strength, keeping them steadfast; after death, He will admit them into Paradise to dwell forever. 

Then comes the highest honor: **(رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ)** (Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him). Allah will be pleased with them because they fulfilled the true requirements of the faith they professed and stood firm through every trial. They displayed exactly the character their Lord desired from His servants, and He will honor them, confirming that He is pleased with their efforts and will grant whatever they desire.

Their being pleased with Him means that all the promises of their Lord, for which they lived and died in this world, will manifest before them in a form far exceeding their expectations and imaginations, leaving them completely fulfilled. Allah concludes: **(أُولَٰئِكَ حِزْبُ اللَّهِ أَلَا إِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ)**. These are the ones who hold the honor of belonging to the Party of Allah, and they are the ones who will attain true success. Having described the traits of both Satan's party and Allah's party, the Surah reaches its conclusion.
